audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences within bprint.c are guarded: ber_int_errno_fn via runtime if-check (L63), ber_pvt_log_print initialized non-NULL at L90 and never nulled within the file, ber_pvt_err_file lazy-initialized to stderr in ber_error_print before its own fputs call (L77), and ber->ber_ptr is guaranteed non-NULL by ber_get_next allocation before ber_log_dump is called from io.c/decode.c. Assert-only guards on data parameters are sufficient because all callers (decode.c, io.c) pass pre-allocated BerElement buffers, not raw attacker-controlled pointers. The ber_pvt_err_file NULL risk when ber_int_log_proc is registered resolves in an external callback, not within bprint.c itself.
